# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Process
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - release/*
jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      packages: write
      pages: write
      id-token: write
      issues: write
      pull-requests: write
      repository-projects: write
      deployments: write
      discussions: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Build packages
        run: npm run build
      - name: Publish packages
        run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
      - name: Create release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
          body: Automated release
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
      - name: Update documentation
        run: |
          echo "Updating documentation..."
          # Add documentation update steps here
      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-artifacts
          path: |
            dist/**
            *.md
            package.json
          retention-days: 7
